flags.concern: The plan violates the required flat step structure. It uses phase headers with nested step headings instead of flat `## Step N:` sections.
flags.evidence: The user explicitly required flat h2 headings (`## Step N:`) with no phase headers pushing steps to h3. The submitted plan uses `## Phase 1/2/3` and `### Step 1/2/...`.

flags.concern: Pathname-only full-screen gating would also hide chrome for the timeline list view on `/tools/video-editor`, which conflicts with the plan's own assumption that only the active editor shell should get the full-screen treatment.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:206-223` serves both states from the same pathname: without `?timeline=...` it renders `TimelineList`, and with `?timeline=...` it renders `VideoEditorShell`. A `pathname === '/tools/video-editor'` check in layout code cannot distinguish those two states.

flags.concern: The full-screen layout steps miss repo-level wrappers that still constrain the editor: the route outlet sits inside a centered `container mx-auto` shell, and the global social footer still renders below the page. Hiding the header and pane padding alone will not produce a true full-screen editor.
flags.evidence: `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:64-67` wraps all routed pages in `<main className="container mx-auto ...">`. `src/app/Layout.tsx:128-134` always renders `SocialIcons`, and `src/app/components/SocialIcons.tsx:9-31` adds vertical footer space. The plan does not account for either wrapper.

flags.concern: The drag plan targets the wrong source component and introduces a parallel drag protocol. Gallery drag starts in `MediaGalleryItem`, not `DraggableImage`, and existing gallery-to-shot drops already use the shared generation drag helpers.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324-326` attaches `draggable` and `onDragStart`, and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:267-273` calls `setGenerationDragData(...)`. `src/shared/components/DraggableImage.tsx:10-15` is only a non-draggable wrapper around children. The shared drag contract lives in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:123-158`, and shot drop targets consume it in `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/components/BatchDropZone.tsx:315-324`.

flags.concern: The plan assumes GenerationsPane items already operate at generation-variant level, but the current pane is backed by project generations, not `generation_variants`. Variant-level drag needs a data-model/query change first, not just extra `dataTransfer` fields.
flags.evidence: `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:67-109` calls `useProjectGenerations(...)` with the normal generations path. In `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts:249-257`, the `generation_variants` query is only used when `filters.variantsOnly` is set. The default generation transform is `transformGeneration` in `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:263-375`; variant rows are only produced by `transformVariant` in `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:438-475`.

flags.concern: The proposed 'register asset' drop flow is not compatible with the current persistence model. `patchRegistry` only mutates local editor state; it does not persist `asset_registry`, and `saveTimeline` writes only timeline config.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts:339-390` shows `patchRegistry(...)` updating local state and calling `commitData(..., { save: false })`. `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:36-52` saves only the `config` column, while `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:87-131` is the existing code path that persists `asset_registry` to Supabase.
